feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Connecting Event Mesh from Cloud Foundry subaccount to CPI(Cloud Integration) in Neo subaccount
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

For our customer, we have SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) in Neo environment and SAP Event Mesh will be in Cloud Foundry Subaccount. Both are in different Global Account.

Could you please let us know whether we can be able to connect seamlessly between two environment (CF and Neo) to integrate SAP CPI and Event Mesh in customer landscape for  Event Driven Integration.

I would like to know the feasibility of this kind of setup and whether there will be any challenges or not?

Regards,

Souvik

entry.title: Resource 'pd:SAP_TPM_xxx:TARGET_MSG_XSD:Binary' not found in Partner Directory
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have an issue with SAP TPM when trying to use Custom Type System. We need custom EDIFACT message which is not available in standard EDIFACT Type System (PRIDES_00A an extended DESADV message used in Finland)

I have created a Custom Type System and created MIG and MAG. But when using the MIG/MAG in agreement TARGET_MAG_XSD is not created in Partner Directory. 

Then i get "Error Details    java.lang.RuntimeException: Resource 'pd:SAP_TPM_xx:TARGET_MSG_XSD:Binary' not found in Partner Directory" when message comes to "XML to EDI Converter"-step in the TPM standerd flow.

Any idea how we can proceed?

Thank you

Regards Brian

entry.title: Error An internal error occurred during message processing. Please check the logs in the Monitor Sys
entry.content:
I have an error when call the following iflow :

 

<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope">
<soap:Body>
<soap:Fault>
<soap:Code>
<soap:Value>soap:Receiver</soap:Value>
</soap:Code>
<soap:Reason>
<soap:Text xml:lang="en">An internal error occurred during message processing. Please check the logs in the Monitor System Logs</soap:Text>
</soap:Reason>
</soap:Fault>
</soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

I check the Monitor System Logs and get two files , however I dont understand the cause.

Other iflows work fine, and the user have the role ESBMessaging

Regards

entry.title: Monitoring Storage optimal usage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 
We are currently using the SAP Integration Suite within our organization, and it is shared among several teams. We are facing challenges with monitoring storage utilization, especially since one team might use more storage than others. The maximum allowed monitoring storage is 35,000 MB. We are also implementing pipeline concepts, which result in 3 or more messages for a single message transfer from sender to receiver.
 
Could you please provide guidance on the following:
1)How can we utilize the monitoring storage wisely and enforce best practices across all teams?
2)What are the potential risks and worst-case scenarios if we exceed the maximum allowed monitoring storage?
3)What strategies can we implement to avoid these risks, especially considering our use of pipeline concepts that generate multiple messages?
We appreciate any insights or recommendations you can offer to help us manage our monitoring storage effectively.
 

Thanks

Ramu.

entry.title: Integration Suite ODATA APIs from Business Hub
entry.content:
Hello all,

I want to create an integration flow, consuming some ODATA services (e.g. the Characteristic Data for Classification -  https://api.sap.com/api/API_CLFN_CHARACTERISTIC_SRV/overview).

I want to start with the sandbox to try out my integration flow. But how can I connect to the sandbox in the integration flow? When using ODATA v2 connection between Request Reply and Recipient, I'm not able to configure the authentication. I can only configure basic authentication with user and password, but to use the sandbox, an API key as authentication method is needed.

How is this meant to work? Or is it not wanted, to use the sandbox as a first (of course read only) test instance?

entry.title: HTTP calls to SAP Cloud Integration from API Management getting rejected - HTTP 500
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am trying to expose a SAP CI api via SAP API management. SAP CI Iflow has a Http Sender Adapter that accepts the payload and pushes it to a JMS Queue.

Inbound requests from postman towards the SAP CI HTTP url using the client id client secret are successful and give a 202 as expected.

When the same API is exposed via API Management it throws a Http 500 , a message log is not created in SAP CI message monitor and there is just a standard response like below.

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
    <title>HTTP Status 500 – Internal Server Error</title>
    <style type="text/css">
        body {
            font-family: Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif;
        }
When i tried to check the logs in SAP CI , I could see that the requests are hitting SAP CI but are getting rejected due to reasons I don't fully understand. 

 

 

SAP API Management has SAP CI as a API Provider - Internet, It's not of type Cloud Integration as I didnt have a working username password and using my suser always throws up a 401 in Test Connection.
Here is my API provider Config - URL is SAP CI Runtime url and not the tenant management url

This is how my debug trace looks. nothing complex here , just getting username password from key value map and then using Basic auth step to place it in the header.

 


  

It's not giving 401 but somehow SAP CI doesnt like the request
Can you please help me understand what the error in the trace means.

Regards

Vinay
#integrationsuite #sapci #sapapimanagement #apimanagement #api #cloudintegration

entry.title: Calling Document Extraction API from Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to develop the interface in the Integration Suite, which at some moment sends a document for the Document Information Extraction service via the following API:

https://xxx.cfapps.xx.hana.ondemand.com/document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs

One of the technical difficulties is the fact that Integration Suite doesn't fully support formdata requests.

After some attempts the following response is received:

{"error": {"code": "E30", "message": "Cannot process file of content type.", "details": [{"code": "0", "message": "Content type: None"}]}}

Request has a Content-Type header with the value:

multipart/form-data; boundary=--cpi

Could you please share some experience or tips and tricks to use of the API from the Integration Suite? Or if you have success stories, would be happy to hear that.

 

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration - Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
I need to provide a user with a role that will allow them to delete messages from a JMS queue.

Following: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/managing-messages-stored-in-queues

I have assigned this user the role of PI_Administrator but they are still unable to delete a message from a JMS queue.

What role will allow this?

entry.title: Configure SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise via Service Key
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am trying to create channel to connect SAP EVENT MESH with S4/Hana On Premise via service key.

While creating channel from /iwxbe/config via service key i am getting below error.

 

Could you please help.

SAP BTP cockpit SAP Integration Suite , SAP Event Mesh 

Thanks & Regards

Deepak

entry.title: Not able to deploy i-Flow
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

 
I'm not able to deploy iFlow in my free tier account, please refer below screenshot.
After I click deploy, I get below error without Deployment Status being unchanged.
Navigate to Manage Integration Content is not available, it remains unclickable.

 

Please help.

 

 
Regards,
Ganesh N

entry.title: Access to APIProxy oData endpoint
entry.content:
0

I have installed the API Proxy packaged on my trail (on us10) instance of SAP Integration suite.

https://api.sap.com/api/APIPortal_APIProxy_CF/overview

It is a proxy to:

https://us10apiportal.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/apiportal/api/1.0/Management.svc

According to the documentation this backend system use oAuth2

But how do this work?

Does it have its own authentication server or do I create one in either SAP BTP Cockpit or as a proxy in the SAP Integration Suite?

If is has it's own, what is my client/secret? If I have to create a auth server myself - how is that linked to the backend system or is it all passed in the token?

I have not been able to find a step be step guide on this.

I'll be pleased if someone can help me on this one.

entry.title: How to transfer header parameters IntegrationSuite to ABAP Server Proxy (XI Adapter) - SAP ERP
entry.content:
Hi community 

we use REST (sender) to ABAP proxy (receiver) interfaces via SAP PI.
Inside server ABAP proxy we access content of SAP PI / ABAP Proxy dynamic configuration parameters aka Adapter Specific Message Attributes (ASMA).
This is a very flexible way of interface design which enables handling generic parameters required by different interfaces independent from specific interface message (payload)

code snippet read dynamic configuration parameters of ABAP Proxy

TRY.
        DATA(lo_srv_context)    =  cl_proxy_access=>get_server_context( ).
        DATA(lr_msg_id_protocol) = CAST if_wsprotocol_message_id( lo_srv_context->get_protocol( if_wsprotocol=>message_id ) ).
        me->mv_msg_id           =  lr_msg_id_protocol->get_message_id( ).

*     Auslesen Aufruf-URL
        DATA(li_internal) = CAST if_wsprotocol_internal( lo_srv_context->get_protocol( 'INTERNAL' ) ).
        DATA(lo_header)   = CAST cl_xms_inbound_proxy_plsrv( li_internal->get_framework( ) )->xmb_message->get_dynamic_header( ).

        me->mt_dyn_conf_table = lo_header->get_records( ).


      CATCH cx_ai_system_fault cx_sy_move_cast_error INTO DATA(lx_system).
        RAISE SHORTDUMP lx_system.
    ENDTRY.

In terms of migration to SAP IntegrationSuite we are looking for a way of reusing the existing ABAP proxy implementation.

Is there a way to provide dynamic configuration parameters with XI receiver adapter in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) or an alternative way to provide header parameters to SAP ERP backend.

If possible we want to avoid redesign backend interface approach. If unavoidable REST interface would be allow flexible header parameters, wright?

Any advise welcome

cheers
Jochen

entry.title: Exploring the Library of Type Systems in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Exploring the Library of Type Systems in SAP Integration Suite 

SAP Integration Suite has the ability to process a wide range of document types, including structured and unstructured data, EDI data, flat files, and database records. To make the message mapping from source to target file easier, SAP Integration Suite provides a robust environment for integrating various systems and services. One of the core components that make the integrations seamless is the library of type systems. In this blog, I’ll dive into the key type systems available and how they can be leveraged to create efficient and scalable integration solutions. 

I’ve created a few MIGs (Message Implementation Guidelines) and MAGs (Mapping Guidelines) using the extensive library of type systems, and it is a lot easier than trying to find or create a schema manually. The library of type systems is a collection of predefined message structures, codelists, etc., provided by agencies that maintain the B2B, A2A, and B2G standards, and is utilized in the creation of message mappings. Each type system is developed and maintained by the agency that owns it, which also makes the creation of the mappings more accurate and reliable.  

The types of message templates that you can currently find in SAP Integration Suite: 

 

ANSI ASC X12 

A set of standards for electronic data interchange (EDI) developed by the Accredited Standards Committee (ASC) X12 and accredited by the American National Standards Institute (ANSI). 

 

ODETTE 

An organization that develops standards, tools, and services to support the digitalization of the European automotive supply chain by providing guidelines for logistics management and supply chain communications. 

 

JAIF 

An organization that promotes the peaceful use of nuclear energy and supports the nuclear industry in Japan, JAIF provides a platform for information exchange, industry collaboration, and policy advocacy related to nuclear energy. 

 

VDA 

The Association of the German Automotive Industry develops standards and guidelines to support the automotive industry, focusing on quality management, production processes, and technical specifications. 

 

UN/EDIFACT 

An international standard for electronic data interchange (EDI) developed by the United Nations, it provides a set of syntax rules, guidelines, and standard messages to facilitate the electronic exchange of structured data between independent computerized information systems across various industries. 

 

cXML / xCBL 

cXML uses XML to provide formal schemas for the exchange of business documents between procurement applications, e-commerce hubs, and suppliers. 

xCBL is a collection of XML specifications for electronic business document exchange. It supports various business processes and industries, facilitating interoperability and automation. 

 

 GS1  

A global organization that develops and maintains standards for supply chain efficiency and visibility. These standards help uniquely identify, accurately capture, and automatically share vital information about products, locations, assets, and more. 

 

ISO 

An independent, non-governmental international organization that develops and publishes standards to ensure the quality, safety, efficiency, and interoperability of products, services, and systems. 

 

SAP SE 

Provides SOAP protocols used for exchanging structured information in the implementation of web services, an open standard for creating and consuming RESTful APIs, and standardized documents used for data exchange between SAP systems and external systems, among others. 

 

Custom Type System 

If you are looking for a specific type system that is not found in the library of type systems, you also have the option to upload custom messages based on your own message structures. 

 

SAP's extensive library of type systems is highly beneficial for creating source and target MIGs – used in the creation of MAGs – for several key reasons: 

  1. Standardization: The use of standardized type systems ensures consistency across different integration scenarios. 
  2.  Interoperability: With a wide range of pre-defined types, SAP's library allows different systems and applications to communicate seamlessly. 
  3.  Reusability: The type systems in SAP's library can be reused across multiple projects and scenarios, reducing the need for custom development. 
  4.  Flexibility and Extensibility: SAP's type systems are designed to be flexible and extensible, meaning they can be easily adapted to meet specific business requirements. 
  5.  Robust Documentation: SAP provides comprehensive documentation for its type systems, which serves as a valuable resource for creating detailed and accurate message implementation guides. 
  6.  Support for Multiple Formats: SAP's type systems support various data formats, making it easier to create message implementation guides and message mappings that cater to different integration needs. 
  7.  Compliance with Industry Standards: Many of SAP's type systems are aligned with industry standards. 

 

Conclusion 

SAP Integration Suite's integrated library of type systems offers a wide range of options to cater to diverse integration needs. The availability of type systems, messages, and code lists and the ability to create MIGs without the need to research or create a given schema for a message type makes the creation of the MAGs much easier and more efficient. I personally find the selection of type systems impressive and look forward to the creation of more messages. 

If you would like to learn more, please Contact Proclus - www.proclus-group.com

entry.title: Apache Kafka Connection from BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement to connect to Apache Kafka from BTP Integration Suite and then transform those XML messages and push them to SAP Backend systems. Could you please let me know if there are any pre-requisites needed to connect to Apache Kafka using Integration Suite. I knew that there is a standard Kafka adapter to connect to Apache Kafka but still any important points to consider before building interface on SAP Integration Suite? We mainly need support to connect to Apache Kafka .. What details should we ask Apache Kafka to share? Should we use connect to Apache Kafka using cloud Connector? Any settings needed to done on Apache Kafka side also? Also should we raise Firewall clearance?

Thanks

Ramu.

entry.title: Unable to view other navigation options in Integration Suite in SAP BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am currently doing the "Developing with SAP Integration Suite" course on SAPlearning. I have activated the trial account of SAP BTP. In the SAP BTP cockpit, I have subscribed to the Integration Suite and assigned some roles related to Process Integration. Still, I can only see the Integration Suite home page and not any other option in the navigation window. Please let me know what I missed.

I cannot proceed doing the hands-on mentioned in further units of this course. Attaching the screenshot for reference (the first image is the home page of SAP BTP cockpit, the second image is the roles I assigned.)

Please assist.

Thanks 

Nikita Agrawal

entry.title: Service Now to Jira Integration - Attachments not working (404 Error Code)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have been integration SNOW to JIRA, but while sending the attachments from SAP CPI to JIRA I am getting the error as XSRF check failed. I have already added the relevant setting in the header but still unable to resolved.

PFA the details of the error and suggest me where I am wrong and how can I resolve the same.

Thanks.

Best Regards,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Groovy Script dynamic time calculation - Error "No such property"
entry.content:
Hi .. We have a requirement to dynamically substract minutes/hours from current time. 

Here is sample Groovy script, working fine in Groovy compiler. but in SAP Iflow, getting error saying that 

"No such property: minutes for class: java.lang.String"

 

import groovy.time.TimeCategory
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat

DateTo = new Date()

use( groovy.time.TimeCategory ) {
def value = 200
DateFrom = DateTo - value.minutes
}

 

 

Could you please help.

Thank you.

entry.title: Simulate Offline mode - Edge integration cell
entry.content:
Hello, Experts.

Recently, we configured the Edge integration cell in AWS Kubernetes. We understand that the EIC can be conducted offline for four hours, as shown in the link below.

Could you guide me through the processes to simulate offline mode for the Edge integration cell?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/frequently-asked-questions-faq-on-edge-integration-cell/ba-p/13571636

Regards

Sebastian Amulraj

entry.title: Interface from MQ to SAP ECC (webservices) via BTP IS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to build interface from MQ to SAP ECC (webservices) using SAP Integration (Cloud Integration). Could you please let me know what should be receiver adapter that should be used to post the data to SAP ECC using webservices URL? Also if someone has already worked on how would be the sample URL look like? because I would need those info to raise FW request, Cloud connector entries .. I mean https://<<host>>:<<port>> ... I mainly interested on port info (is it 8080 or something else) and this would help me before we start developing artifacts..

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Query using the service of Northwind of ODATA
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I am learing Integration Suite and for this I am doing a query using the service of Northwind of ODATA as follow:

https://services.odata.org/V2/Northwind/Northwind.svc/Orders?&$select=OrderID,CustomerID,EmployeeID,OrderDate,RequiredDate,ShippedDate,ShipVia,Freight,ShipName,ShipAddress,ShipCity,ShipRegion,ShipPostalCode,ShipCountry 

I want that appear as follow:

And appear as follow:

 
What do I need to make it appear the way I want?

Regards
